topic.what: Visual memory poisoning attack exploiting long-term memory persistence in agentic recommender systems
topic.how: Injects adversarial visual triggers into uploaded images that act as sleeper agents, hijacking agent reasoning during future planning retrieval without explicit prompt injection
topic.why_matters: Reveals critical attack surface in Agentic RecSys that relies on persistent memory; existing defenses like provenance tracking and input/output filtering provide limited protection

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success in steering final recommendation toward adversary-defined goal
characteristics.context: Memory-attributed goal hits via semantic similarity >0.75 threshold against undefended LLaMA-3.2-Vision-90B agent

characteristics.what_it_measures: Strict attribution-aware success rate counting only poisoned-memory-mediated goal hits
characteristics.evidence: Table 1; validated via counterfactual, plan-trace auditing, and human verification (kappa=0.81)
characteristics.context: Stricter than GHR; excludes cases where hit may come from genuine query-catalog relevance

characteristics.what_itasures: ASR-M when attacking with surrogate encoder against different target encoder
characteristics.evidence: Protocol P1 Table 5 off-diagonal entries across CLIP/SigLIP/OpenCLIP
characteristics.context: Black-box attack transfer under static-unseen protocol; P2 realistic setting yields 65.7% for representative pair

characteristics.what_it_measures: Human-assessed perceptual indistinguishability of poisoned images
characteristics.evidence: Human ratings on 5-point scale; images imperceptibly perturbed at epsilon=8/255
characteristics.context: Tradeoff with attack effectiveness; higher epsilon increases ASR but reduces stealth

characteristics.what_it_measures: Per-request latency added by CognitiveGuard defense
characteristics.evidence: Table 1; Appendix B.1 profiled pipeline with k=5 memories, LLaMA-90B
characteristics.context: Excludes upload-time 0.3s one-time preprocessing; adaptive selective verification can reduce further

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rate of benign memories incorrectly flagged as adversarial
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs 45.2% ASR-M reduction with System 1 alone

characteristics.what_it_measures: Semantic alignment of agent reasoning chains
characteristics.evidence: Table 1; measures cosine similarity between planning outputs
characteristics.context: Higher with defense indicates agent reasoning not corrupted by filtered memories

characteristics.what_it_measures: Threshold for flagging memories as pivot points
characteristics.vs_baseline: Calibrated on 200 human-annotated samples achieving 0.89 correlation
characteristics.context: Default; calibrated threshold balances detection vs false positives

novelty: First formalization of visual memory poisoning as sleeper agent attack in agentic systems
Dual-process defense framework combining diffusion purification (System 1) with counterfactual reasoning verification (System 2)
Introduction of Inception effect: dormant-activation-hijacking-persistence four-stage attack lifecycle
Novel metrics: Goal-Hit Rate (GHR) and Memory-mediated Attack Success Rate (ASR-M) for attributing attacks to poisoned memory
open_problems: Robustness under extreme distribution shifts (new product categories, multilingual queries)
Joint optimization attacks targeting both low RDS and high coherence (achieves only 18.4% ASR-M, fundamental tradeoff)
Healthcare OOD setting shows F1 degradation of -0.15 without recalibration
Heterogeneous encoder consensus degrades recommendation quality despite lowering ASR-M
Attacks may degrade significantly when actual query distribution differs substantially from sampled distribution
